evals.prompt: I just made a new empty directory ~/Projects/log-analyzer and want to set it up as a Python project that I'll work on with both Claude Code and Cursor. I want AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, rules files for both tools, and a local ISSUES.md tracker (it's a personal project, not going on GitHub). Walk me through it.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs full project-init protocol: Phase 0 ingest, Phase 1 plan with prefix strategy choice, Phase 2 scaffold with uv-based pyproject.toml, Phase 3 generates AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md + both rules-file sets + ISSUES.md, Phase 4 review, Phase 5 handoff to dev-workflow with 1-3 seeded issues. Should_trigger: true.

evals.prompt: In our existing Flask app (already has AGENTS.md, tests, CI — all set up) I want to add a new /users/{id}/preferences endpoint. How do we do this?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes the project is already initialized — this is ongoing development, NOT scaffolding. project-init should not trigger; dev-workflow is correct. Should_trigger: false.

evals.prompt: I have a 3-page design doc in docs/design.md for a data pipeline that ingests CSV files from S3, validates them, and loads into Postgres. The directory is otherwise empty. Can you bootstrap a project around this design — I want it to be AI-collaborative from day 1.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs project-init starting from Phase 0 ingest of design.md, decomposes into action nodes in Phase 1, scaffolds Python+uv project structure in Phase 2, generates full documentation hierarchy in Phase 3 referencing the design doc. Should_trigger: true.
